description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular, stacked-stone base and a polygonal stone platform extending outward. The well is topped with a pitched, shingled wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden posts. A wooden crank wheel is mounted on one side, connected to a horizontal axle above the well opening, suggesting a mechanism for drawing water. The interior of the well is open and appears deep, with the stone lining continuing downward. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for historical or rural urban settings.
description: A rustic stone water well with a shingled wooden roof and a visible crank mechanism, featuring a circular stone base and open interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, shingled roof, and the side-mounted crank wheel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the wooden crank wheel and the open well interior beneath the pitched roof.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the crank wheel partially visible on the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the back of the roof and the supporting wooden post, with the crank mechanism less visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden shingled roof supported by posts; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Wooden crank wheel mechanism mounted above the well opening; no visible bucket.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall extending above ground level, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
